entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • FPDA countries say they want to give 50-year-old defence pact 'modern relevance'
  • Ramsay Hunt syndrome caused Justin Bieber's facial paralysis; world tour dates affected
  • Triple Olympic champion Wlodarczyk injured while foiling attempted break-in
  • Shanghai kicks off new round of mass COVID-19 testing, Beijing cases jump
  • North Korean leader reaffirms arms buildup in party meeting
  • China becoming more 'coercive and aggressive', says US defence chief
  • McIlroy charges but Clark clings to Canadian Open lead
  • Is 'fashionably late' out of fashion? Has punctuality become mainstream during the pandemic?
  • Is it unhealthy to keep secrets? What can you do if you need to talk about them?
  • David Beckham's coming to Singapore on Jun 17: Think you'll be lucky enough to meet him?
  • Soft toy hospitals: Meet the 'doctors' who repair childhood plushies in Singapore
  • K-pop group BTS promotes new album with New York pop-up shop
  • At least S$25 million lost to China official impersonation scammers since January 2022: Police
  • Justin Bieber says he's working to recover from partial face paralysis
  • Olympic marathon champion Jepchirchir targeting 2023 world record attempt
  • Snorkelling with whale sharks, underwater dining and meeting a heron named George in the Maldives
  • Canada, in a world first, proposes health warnings on individual cigarettes
  • One million flowers bring joy and color to New York's Meatpacking District
  • Biden promised Bolsonaro US would reconsider tariffs on Brazil steel
  • Father and son take Isle of Man TT deaths to five for 2022
  • Commentary: Malaysia has one competitive edge left if it wants to capitalise on global palm oil shortage
  • IN FOCUS: 'I have great fear' - Despair and anger as Sri Lanka plunges deeper into crisis
  • Brazilian suspect held for 30 days in case of missing British journalist
  • France in no mood to make concessions to Russia, presidency says
  • Death penalty in Malaysia remains, but will no longer be mandatory for some cases: PM Ismail Sabri
  • China will 'not hesitate to start war' over Taiwan, Beijing tells US
  • Kishida unveils shift in diplomacy to meet external challenges faced by Japan
  • Asteroid samples contain 'clues to origin of life': Japan scientists
  • Hong Kong's status undermined by COVID-19 curbs, leader Carrie Lam says
  • China calls COVID-19 'lab leak' theory a lie after WHO report
  • Shots fired, three injured in Hong Kong triad clash
  • Amid South China Sea tensions, US and China should better hear ASEAN’s voice: Indonesia’s Prabowo
  • 'We must change': Japan's morning-after pill debate
  • China's Xi backs zero-COVID policy as Shanghai expands mass testing
  • Malaysia government agrees to abolish mandatory death penalty
  • Apple's app store to allow more payment options for Dutch dating app developers
  • Tesla to seek investor approval for 3-for-1 stock split
  • Biden says ExxonMobil 'made more money than God'
  • Meta probing Sheryl Sandberg's use of company resources - WSJ
  • CPPIB, GIC did not participate in Eletrobras privatization -sources
  • Netflix shares slide as Goldman downgrades on grim economic picture
  • Activision Blizzard recognizes new Call of Duty workers union
  • Messaging app Telegram to launch paid subscription plan
  • Apple's Cook urges US lawmakers to pass federal privacy law
  • Pakistan unveils 2022/23 budget, aims for 5% growth
  • Taiwan says US understands its exchange rate policy stance
  • Dutch government will not sell land to Facebook parent Meta -ANP
  • Researchers reveal secret find of 340-year-old sunken royal warship
  • Former chief financial officer gets jail for insider trading, acquiring benefits from criminal conduct
  • Explainer: What are the consequences of the yen's fall to a 20-year low?
  • Analysis-Buy Now Pay Later business model faces test as rates rise
  • Crypto regulation efforts need to keep pace with market growth -Bank of Canada official
  • China's top livestreamer Li Jiaqi's online silence stretches to a week
  • A walk in Singapore’s nature reserve led a researcher to discover the rare flower of a critically endangered tree
  • Singapore’s media played an ‘honourable, rational and essential role’ throughout COVID-19 pandemic: Josephine Teo
  • Singapore to contribute humanitarian assistance package to Ukraine: MFA
  • COVID-19 treatment subsidies to be scaled back as Singapore progressively returns to normal: MOH
  • Timeline: Events leading up to the Government’s takeover of Singapore Sports Hub
  • Singapore and South Korea sign 2 MOUs on defence, media cooperation
  • Capacity limits, COVID-19 tests no longer needed at nightlife establishments from Jun 14
  • People aged 50 and above may get second COVID-19 booster at vaccination centres with mRNA shots: MOH
  • Migrant workers living in dormitories have to apply for visit pass to go to 4 locations on Sundays, public holidays
  • Five on Friday: 5 worst types of people to be stuck in a traffic jam with
  • Largest cultivated chicken meat facility in Asia breaks ground in Singapore
  • Plans to make Sports Hub a 'community icon' with more sports and lifestyle activities
  • SportSG will take over ownership of Singapore Sports Hub to make it more accessible to the community
  • Timeline: How a prominent Catholic figure’s sex abuse offences against teenage boys came to light
  • Changi Airport's Terminal 4 to reopen in September this year
  • SCDF unveils training facility with five labs to enhance emergency responders' physical, mental performance
  • Bulls stun Leinster in Dublin to earn URC final berth
  • Pasalic pounces to give Croatia 1-0 over Denmark
  • Super sub Mbappe salvages draw for France against Austria
  • Nawaz picks four as Pakistan beat West Indies to seal ODI series
  • Schwartzel leads chase for $4 million LIV jackpot
  • Molano disqualified after hitting rider twice in Criterium du Dauphine
  • Tiger joins LeBron, Jordan on sport billionaire list: Forbes
  • Bale warns of 'crazy' demands on players
  • Cheers! Mitchell makes a splash with a six into fan's beer glass

  • Big-hitting DeChambeau becomes latest to join LIV Golf
  • Co-producer Hamilton says he will not appear in Brad Pitt F1 film
  • Foden, Phillips doubtful for England's Nations League game against Italy
  • FIFA dismiss Chile complaint, Ecuador to keep spot at World Cup

  • Commentary: Will Hollywood still want Johnny Depp or Amber Heard movies after this frenzied trial?
  • Canada to suspend random COVID-19 testing to reduce airport wait times
  • Greek PM says EU must integrate Western Balkans by 2033: Report
  • Britain, UN official condemn Donbas death sentences against British soldiers
  • US expects 300,000 more Jynneos doses as monkeypox cases jump to 45
  • 'Girls' education is a climate solution': Malala Yousafzai joins climate protest

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Idaho City to Pay $11.7 Million to Man Wrongfully Convicted in 1996 Killing

entry.title: Guardian: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • NBA finals Game 4: Golden State Warriors v Boston Celtics – live!

  • Ministers want Britain to be more like Netflix: debt-ridden and fast losing fans | Marina Hyde

  • One in 500 men carry extra sex chromosome, research suggests
  • How was the first January 6 hearing? Our panel weighs in | Panel

  • Lost photos from Spanish civil war reveal daily life behind anti-fascist lines
  • ‘What a ball-buster!’ Juno Temple on sass, Ted Lasso and taking on the mafia

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 11 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-11 03:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Three steps for Hong Kong to reconnect with the world and regain its international lustre
  • More than 540,000 Hongkongers issued BN(O) passports since 2019 with most approved after UK citizenship scheme unveiled
  • Hong Kong unveils strategy for containing possible monkeypox outbreak but shies away from travel bans
  • Native-speaking English teachers in Hong Kong government schools told to declare allegiance to city or face dismissal
  • Hong Kong court tells former ESF worker accused of taking bribes for kindergarten placements to surrender travel documents
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong police arrest three more people for submitting fake Covid-19 results to authorities as city logs 672 new cases
  • Hong Kong’s New World Development sells US$700 million dual-tranche green, social bond in global first
  • Opposition veteran Yeung Sum expresses no regret over loss of Hong Kong government honours alongside two peers
  • Research co-led by CityU boosts efficiency of perovskite solar cells to record high
  • Can Hong Kong become a ‘legal hub’? State official urges city to lean on strengths to aid country’s ‘foreign-related’ work in field, such as in treaties and international legislation
  • Implementing core values from the start at YCIS
  • China’s hostile environment for Western tech pushed out Amazon and Airbnb, but competition remains the biggest challenge
  • US defence chief Lloyd Austin says China is getting ‘more coercive’ in its territorial claims
  • Covid-19 data points to animal origin but more studies needed to spot what and where: WHO advisers
  • Senior China brass rebuts Kishida’s claim Beijing, not Tokyo, disrupted East China Sea status quo
  • Ukraine war casts shadow over China’s belt and road ties with Russia-led Eurasian trade bloc
  • TikTok’s workplace controversy in London marks the latest culture clash as China’s Big Tech ventures overseas
  • China puts out ‘most detailed’ map of the moon’s surface
  • As tensions rise, China’s delegates are likely to keep a low profile at the Shangri-La Dialogue
  • China chip makers scramble for semiconductor talent, showering fresh graduates with offers as peers in other fields face dim prospects
  • China’s AI drone vessel takes maiden voyage, passes first sea trial
  • Taiwan Covid-19 deaths hit record high as new cases continue to drop
  • As China’s capital reopens restaurants, foodies and lunch crowd remain reluctant to dine out
  • Tencent rolls out simplified employee rating system amid job cuts, other cost-saving measures
  • China, US defence chiefs square off on Taiwan, Ukraine in first face-to-face talks
  • China’s new bank loans nearly triple in May as Beijing steps up policy support
  • Shanghai warns public to avoid crowds as it prepares to test 14 million residents for coronavirus on Saturday
  • ‘Angel particle’ mimics may be first step to reliable quantum computers
  • China’s gaokao university entrance exam: family celebrates end of daughter’s test with banner declaring she can now ‘fall in love’
  • Climate change: why multinational firms, eager to buy green power in China, find it hard to get enough to meet their decarbonisation goals
  • Tencent pulls plug on Twitch-like platform Penguin Esports as regulators freeze out the social media giant from new gaming licences
  • China diplomat outlines complicated causes of Sri Lanka’s debt crisis
  • Chinese help for Cambodian naval base does not mean PLA troops on the ground, say analysts
  • Marcos touts Beijing ties as Philippines issues new South China Sea protest
  • North Korea appoints first woman foreign minister as Kim doubles down on arms buildup
  • China’s ‘walking on two legs’ conundrum: how to balance zero-Covid and the economy?
  • As Chinese buyer drops US$61m on 20 condo units, what’s fuelling Singapore’s home prices?
  • We live in the age of nasty metaverse wars. Is there no escape from this hell?
  • World’s poorest nations suffering the fallout of a heating planet need cash now, UN climate talks
  • Anger erupts in Bangladesh and India as thousands protest over comments about Islam
  • Singapore to open Asia’s biggest lab-grown meat facility in another world-first
  • Bon Odori festival saga sheds light on how Malaysia governs Islam, after sultan rebukes federal minister
  • Singapore’s Changi airport to reopen terminals as travel recovers faster than expected
  • Waste of energy: why Japan’s gamble on power since Fukushima could mean a hot summer, rolling blackouts, and the restart of nuclear plants
  • First in Asia, Thailand makes marijuana legal, but smoking discouraged
  • Malaysia to scrap mandatory death penalty, giving judges sentencing discretion
  • Coronavirus: Thailand ready to scrap last of pandemic-era curbs on tourists
  • Johnny Depp’s ‘US$62,000 bill’ at UK Indian restaurant after winning Amber Heard defamation case spotlights Hollywood’s curry mania
  • IPL: Amazon’s Jeff Bezos, India’s Mukesh Ambani, Disney and Sony to battle over US$7.7 billion rights to Super Bowl of cricket
  • Brazil police find apparent human remains in river where UK reporter vanished
  • Canadian singer Justin Bieber says he is suffering from facial paralysis
  • UK’s Boris Johnson had a bad week. Here are the next potential flashpoints
  • Ukraine pleads for more weapons, as cholera spreads
  • US dropping Covid-19 tests for incoming air travel
  • Ukraine: Chronic hunger for 19 million more over next year due to war, UN food agency warns
  • UK judge rejects emergency bid to stop asylum seekers from being sent to Rwanda
  • After Covid-19 and monkeypox, more animal disease threats to come, warn experts
  • TikTok adds ‘screen time breaks’ for addicts unable to stop scrolling
  • ‘War scene’: five key moments from the US Capitol riot hearing
  • Ukraine now dependent on arms from allies after exhausting Soviet-era weaponry
  • Biden and Brazil’s Bolsonaro hold first meeting: some nice words, but distant
  • Tesla proposes 3-to-1 stock split as Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, friend of Elon Musk, prepares to leave board
  • Coronavirus lockdowns cost China’s car sector another 400,000 vehicles in May, though ‘revenge spending’ may soon provide a boost
  • Global hunt for Russian billionaires’ superyachts won’t end Ukraine war
  • Will India benefit as China’s Covid-19 lockdowns hit Apple supply chains?
  • HSBC, Standard Chartered must address ‘shortcomings’ in plans for a potential bank failure: Bank of England
  • Former CSRC official Feng Henian under investigation as Beijing’s anti-corruption crackdown continues
  • Huobi Tech to expand virtual asset ecosystem on back of strong results
  • Why global economies should expect to see out 2022 in better shape than now
  • How global warming sparked pair’s sustainable architecture with its focus on natural ventilation and high-rise living connected to nature
  • ‘Historical nihilists’ know that the truth ultimately seeps through, eroding that which attempts to conceal it
  • Singapore’s Gillman Barracks arts centre to become lifestyle destination after several major galleries give up their leases
  • Is Japanese manga One Piece ending after 25 years? Comic’s creator says he’s taking a break to work on its ‘final chapter’
  • BTS launch Proof, a triple album of new and old songs, with promise that the best is ‘Yet to Come’
  • With Dior collaboration and exposure from A$AP Rocky, Erl – fashion label that’s a 2022 LVMH Prize winner – is on the up. Its founder takes stock
  • Jhumpa Lahiri, American writer, on her ‘Italian years’ and the freedom adopting another language has given her
  • Happy Hour movie review: Drive My Car director Ryusuke Hamaguchi’s 2015 masterpiece is a five-hour meditation on friendship, marriage and connection
  • Can German food find love in Hong Kong? Restaurants offer modern takes on traditional dishes – it’s not all sausages and sauerkraut
  • Meet Princess Märtha Louise of Norway: newly engaged to Gwyneth Paltrow’s spirit guide Durek Verrett, the 50-year-old royal claims to talk to horses and once opened an ‘angel school’
  • Inside Melania Trump’s US$100,000 shoe collection: from Valentino and Christian Louboutin, to Jimmy Choo and Manolo Blahnik, she’s got 130 pairs in New York alone …
  • Who is Britney’s low-key brother Bryan Spears? The ‘only’ family member invited to her secret wedding to Sam Asghari once said his sister’s conservatorship was ‘a great thing’ ...
  • Who’s the richest Knowles-Carter? The family’s net worths, ranked: from Jay-Z and Beyoncé’s billion-dollar empire, to Solange’s solo success and Kelly Rowland and Michelle Williams’ Destiny’s Child millions
  • Who is Rebel Wilson’s new girlfriend, Ramona Agruma? The Pitch Perfect actress came out as LGBT during Pride Month, and she’s dating a fashion designer and owner of sustainable brand Lemon Ve Limon
  • Who is Jason Alexander, Britney Spears’ wedding crasher? Her first husband was arrested at her secret ceremony to Sam Asghari – and is a Trump supporter spotted at the US Capitol riot
  • The 10 best bars in Hong Kong: from Four Seasons’ Argo, The Mandarin Oriental’s The Aubrey and cocktail hotspot Penicillin, to OG molecular mixologist Quinary, Rosewood’s DarkSide and speakeasy 001
  • Is Aespa the new Blackpink? The K-pop supergroups, compared – from Karina, Giselle, Winter and Ningning’s Coachella performances and endorsements with luxury brands like Givenchy, to smash hit debuts
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Akihito, Michiko view exhibition commemorating Okinawa’s return
  • Nihon Hidankyo picks new co-chair to replace late Tsuboi
  • COVID-19 cases mark 28th day of week-on-week decline in Tokyo
  • ‘Kamikaze car’ attacks signal resumption of yakuza turf war
  • Tokyo ward estimates 1.3% of residents are ‘hikikomori’
  • Shionogi says continuing Vietnam COVID projects after partner scandals
  • Ishikane: Japan must be a bridge for U.N. Security Council members
  • Yoon: Plans under way for Kishida talks at NATO summit

  • Ramen company makes dish free of gluten to sell in United States
  • U.S., Chinese defence chiefs stand firm over Taiwan in first meeting
  • Chinese fighter jet crashes into homes, killing 1 on ground
  • Shanghai faces unexpected round of COVID testing for most residents
  • Flooding, landslides in China leave 17 dead, 4 missing
  • U.S. approves $120 million sale to maintain Taiwanese warships
  • Myanmar court gives go-ahead for Australian’s secrets trial
  • French lawmakers pledge support for Taiwan on island visit
  • Australia, New Zealand unite on China's Pacific threat
  • U.S. official meets Marcos in Philippines in diplomatic push
  • U.N. elects new council members including Japan, Switzerland
  • Iran pulls UN nuke cameras in possible ‘fatal blow’ to deal
  • Sample from asteroid dates to soon after birth of solar system
  • Murakami opens up on his latest translation of F. Scott Fitzgerald
  • Okinawa ‘soba’ delicacy wins over tastebuds in Brazilian city
  • Disneyland to launch rebuilt Space Mountain ride in 2027
  • EDITORIAL: Government must start now to tackle plunging number of births
  • VOX POPULI: Leaders deserve to be judged for trying, not just failed diplomacy
  • VOX POPULI: Cheap Philippine bananas come at the expense of their producers
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
